title: LittlePrince_MEG_French_Read_Pallier2025
description: This dataset comprises magnetoencephalography (MEG) recordings from 50 healthy French-speaking adults reading the French text of *Le Petit Prince* presented via rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP). Each participant completed 9 experimental runs with words displayed at 225 ms duration and 50 ms inter-word intervals. The dataset includes high-resolution anatomical MRI scans, preprocessed MEG data with source reconstructions, and FreeSurfer cortical surface models, enabling investigation of neural mechanisms underlying word recognition and reading comprehension.
methods_description: MEG data were recorded using a whole-head Elekta Neuromag TRIUX system (102 magnetometers, 204 planar gradiometers) at 1000 Hz sampling rate with online filtering (0.1–330 Hz). Vertical and horizontal electrooculograms and electrocardiograms were recorded simultaneously. High-resolution T1-weighted anatomical MRI scans were acquired on a 3T Siemens Magnetom Prisma using MPRAGE sequence for coregistration and cortical surface reconstruction. Stimuli were presented using RSVP with words displayed individually (225 ms duration, 50 ms inter-word interval, 500 ms sentence-final pause) across 9 runs (mean duration 8 min 10 s).
